title: Cirrus: Push snap continuously
body:
A continuation of #3889.

This needs a login file generated with `snapcraft export-login` and then pasted into `SNAPCRAFT_LOGIN` [encrypted variable](https://cirrus-ci.org/guide/writing-tasks/#encrypted-variables).

comments.body:
Hi @abitrolly. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
@rhatdan @cevich `snapcraft push` won't pass without valid credentials.
/ok-to-test
@abitrolly Not sure what I have to do, hopefully @cevich knows.
looking...
... oh okay, yep I can help with that.  @abitrolly is this a free service I (or you) can simply create robot (a.k.a. service-account) credentials, then I can encrypt those for cirrus?

> @rhatdan @cevich `snapcraft push` won't pass without valid credentials.

@abitrolly @cevich wonder if a separate account like I use (lsm5-bot) for launchpad builds can help, so you won't have to risk your main account.

okay, poked around, read some stuff, I think I understand generally how this is intended to work.  To answer my own earlier question-  It's probably **not** a good idea to encode personal credentials for this use-case.  Mostly because:

1. They could be leaked and misused before anyone notices.
2. The person owning them might go on vacation at an inopportune moment.

Which presents a challenge, as it seems there are no provisions for service-accounts or group-owned accounts in Ubuntu One (the SSO backing snapcraft).  I'll need to discuss this with the team so we can figure out what to do that's most useful for everyone.
@lsm5 ahhh! you beat me to the 'submit' button lol :laughing:
/hold
Adding /hold as a reminder to add an `only_if` at the last possible moment.  We want this `upload_snap_task` to only run post-merge on a branch, not for every PR.

Credentials conclusion: I'm working on setting up a dedicated automation account we can use for this purpose that the team can own - so there's no one person with the keys.  I'll get you the encrypted value as soon as that's ready.
Update: @lsm5 and I have setup a robot account and are finalizing access to the snap "name".
Awesome work.

@abitrolly okay, all set.  Stick this in and snapcraft should start working:

```yaml
...cut...
    env:
        SNAPCRAFT_LOGIN: ENCRYPTED[d8e82eb31c6372fec07f405f413d57806026b1a9f8400033531ebcd54d6750a5e4a8b1f68e3ec65c98c65e0d9b2a6a75]
...cut...
```

Please let me or @lsm5 know if you need help.
Rebased and added DCO. Not sure what's wrong with CI.

[+0300s] AssertionError: 'upload_snap' not found in {'special_testing_endpoint', 'verify_test_built_images', 'testing', 'image_prune', 'vendor', 'special_testing_in_podman', 'build_each_commit', 'varlink_api', 'build_without_cgo', 'meta', 'test_building_snap', 'special_testing_cgroupv2', 'testing_crun', 'test_build_cache_images', 'special_testing_rootless', 'special_testing_cross', 'gating'} : Please add "upload_snap" to the "depends_on" list in "success_task"
Should be fixed now. Why not to move CI setup from OCI runtime unit tests to `success` task?
@cevich PTAL ^^

One thing we talked about was potentially having multiple SNAPS.
Basically one equivalent to 
* Rawhide, in that each time something gets merged into libpod we would cut a new package.
* Release, Each time we cut a release on Github, a new Release package gets put into snap.

Can we do this?

> One thing we talked about was potentially having multiple SNAPS.
> Basically one equivalent to
> 
>     * Rawhide, in that each time something gets merged into libpod we would cut a new package.
> 
>     * Release, Each time we cut a release on Github, a new Release package gets put into snap.
> 
> 
> Can we do this?

Yes, snaps have multiple channels, edge (like rawhide), dev/beta (not sure of the names, but kinda like -testing) and stable.

(Side note) I noticed I can edit comments posted by other people (I guess others can too), which is scary.
@lsm5 @cevich I would like you guys to approve this and get it merged in.

> @lsm5 @cevich I would like you guys to approve this and get it merged in.

I've been out of the snap loop for a while. It seems `test_building_snap` is successful. @cevich is that sufficient guarantee that a snap will be pushed out?
LGTM, but definitely want head nods from cevich and @lsm5
@cevich even

> Should be fixed now. Why not to move CI setup from OCI runtime unit tests to success task?

Oh I understand what you mean, yes, that's a good idea...#4073

> Can we do this?

YES!  Thanks for reminding me. What I was thinking is the publishing task can check `$CIRRUS_TAG`  -  it's only non-empty if there's a tag associated with the code it's currently running.  

In other words, since the publishing task only runs during post-merge anyway:
* No tag: publish to the `edge` channel
* Tag: publish to the `beta`
* When we get something we like, manually tag from `beta` -> `stable`

(or something to this affect)

I'm also cool if we want to implement this part as a followup PR.

/approve
/lgtm

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Ok I am going to merge, and then we can get follow on PR to publish on the correct channels.
Thanks @abitrolly
Rebased again because of merge conflicts.

***Note*** After this merges, it would be wise to monitor the post-merge execution to confirm the snap package was build and uploaded - since it only happens during post-merge.

https://cirrus-ci.com/github/containers/libpod/master
/hold cancel
/lgtm

> **_Note_** After this merges, it would be wise to monitor the post-merge execution to confirm the snap package was build and uploaded - since it only happens during post-merge.
>
> https://cirrus-ci.com/github/containers/libpod/master

I don't see `upload_snap` task at all - https://cirrus-ci.com/build/6163886895529984. No idea how to debug it.

Yep, that's exactly the problem, it should be there.  I'm tracking this problem in https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1915#issuecomment-534724327
(as you already noticed)
